preset_description: Dark = identity signal. Visual language expresses who the user is. Validator gets authority and weight (heavy dark). Mover gets minimal light with one dark CTA punch. Explorer gets all-neutral — open, spacious, no urgency.

brief.audience: SaaS trial Users for an Intent-Based, AI-Ready Design System

sections.name: Global Navigation
sections.rationale: Every page begins with NavigationHeader. For a Validator archetype at the awareness stage, the full variant is appropriate — it provides wayfinding context and signals a complete, trustworthy product ecosystem. The persistent CTA in nav serves as a constant conversion anchor for trial users evaluating whether to commit.

sections.name: Hero — Credibility-Led Opening Statement
sections.rationale: At the awareness stage, Validators need immediate context and a reason to trust before they engage further. HeroPrimary (Validator variant with trust_signal) establishes the value proposition with embedded credibility. HeroStatement (Validator variant with-eyebrow) is also a strong candidate — anchoring authority before the headline lands suits a trial user who is still forming their opinion of the product. Both components explicitly support Validator archetype and awareness funnel stage.

sections.name: Social Proof — Immediate Trust Signal
sections.rationale: Validators at the awareness stage need credibility signals early — before they invest attention in features or arguments. SocialProofBar (Validator variant leading with a quote and logos below) placed directly after the hero satisfies the Validator's need for third-party validation. This component explicitly supports Validator archetype and awareness funnel stage, and best-practice guidance confirms it should drop immediately below the hero on awareness pages.

sections.name: Narrative Argument — Why This System Exists
sections.rationale: HeroStatement can serve double duty as a mid-page narrative section for Validators at the awareness stage who need to understand product philosophy before evaluating features. Trial users deciding whether to pay need a structured argument — not just a feature list — that explains why an intent-based, AI-ready design system matters. The Validator variant's with-eyebrow anchors each claim in credibility. Both HeroStatement and HeroPrimary support Validator + awareness.

sections.name: Secondary Social Proof — Featured Testimonial or Quote
sections.rationale: A second trust touchpoint reinforces the Validator's confidence arc. At the awareness stage, SocialProofBar (Validator variant with a different quote or logo set) provides layered credibility. Placing it after the narrative argument means the user encounters proof right when they're weighing the claims just made. SocialProofBar explicitly supports Validator archetype and awareness funnel stage.

sections.name: Page Close
sections.rationale: Every page ends with FooterMinimal. For a Validator at the awareness stage, the with-cta variant is ideal — it provides a recovery CTA for trial users who scrolled the entire page without converting, giving them one last low-pressure prompt. The standard footer also maintains trust through visible legal text and brand consistency.

page.reasoning: minimal variant for conversion-focused pages — strip nav_links, keep logo and single CTA only. Awareness stage requires focused conversion intent.
Validator variant always includes trust_signal. with-social-proof variant places LogoStrip immediately below CTACluster, establishing credibility for trial users in awareness stage.

slots.headline: The Design System That Knows What You're Building
slots.subhead: MODE adapts components to user intent in real time — reducing design debt by 41% and cutting handoff cycles to under 9 minutes. Trusted by 1,200+ SaaS teams.
slots.trust_signal: No credit card required. Cancel anytime.

page.reasoning: Validator variant leads with quote, logos below. This combination maximizes credibility signals needed to convert skeptical trial users.

slots.headline: Trusted by product teams shipping faster
slots.quote: We cut our component QA time by 38% in the first sprint after switching to MODE. The intent-aware logic alone justified the upgrade.
slots.attribution: Priya Nair, Head of Product Design, Stackwell — 2024 customer survey

page.section: Narrative Argument — Why This System Exists
page.reasoning: Validator variant uses with-eyebrow to anchor credibility before headline lands. This supports the intent-aware positioning and builds trust before deeper narrative.

slots.headline: Static Design Systems Don't Understand Context
slots.subhead: Most systems give you components. MODE gives you components that respond to what users are actually trying to do.
slots.body: Generic design systems treat every interaction the same. MODE's intent layer reads behavioral signals — scroll depth, session history, feature usage — and surfaces the right UI patterns at the right moment. In a 2024 internal study, teams using intent-aware components saw a 2.3x improvement in feature adoption within 30 days of launch. AI-readiness isn't a roadmap item for MODE — it's the foundation every component is built on.

page.section: Secondary Social Proof — Featured Testimonial or Quote
page.reasoning: Validator archetype prefers variants containing social-proof. single-quote provides targeted testimonial credibility to reinforce conversion intent.

slots.quote: MODE replaced three separate tooling layers we were maintaining. Our engineers stopped filing design inconsistency bugs within two weeks. The data speaks for itself — 52% fewer UI-related tickets since rollout.
slots.attribution: Daniel Osei, VP Engineering, Loopline SaaS — G2 verified review, Q1 2025

page.reasoning: with-cta variant recaptures departing users on key conversion pages — essential for awareness stage validators who may exit without completing trial signup.
